  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples

UDN Helper Routines
[Tilera Multicore Components (TMC) Library]

Detailed Description

Simple packets can be sent between tiles over the UDN.

The User Dynamic Network (UDN) provides hardware for routing data packets between CPUs. Each packet starts with two header words, specifying the CPU to which the packet should be routed and which 'demux queue' should receive the packet when it arrives. When packets arrive at the destination CPU, they are sorted into one of four possible demux queues, and the receiving CPU issues tmc_udn0_receive(), tmc_udn1_receive(), or similar calls to pull data words from a particular queue.

Transferring Data

TMC provides convenience functions for sending and receiving data packets. To send a five-word packet to demux queue two on a remote CPU, do the following:

 DynamicHeader header = tmc_udn_header_from_cpu(dest_cpu);
 tmc_udn_send_5(header, UDN2_DEMUX_TAG, data0, data1, data2, data3, data4);

When this packet arrives at the destination CPU, the UDN hardware strips off the header and tag information and places the five data words into demux queue 2. To receive those five data words, do the following:

 data0 = tmc_udn2_receive();
 data1 = tmc_udn2_receive();
 data2 = tmc_udn2_receive();
 data3 = tmc_udn2_receive();
 data4 = tmc_udn2_receive();

For convenience, tmc_udn_send_buffer() and tmc_udn0_receive_buffer() (and the like) can be used to send and receive blocks of memory. Using these calls is particularly useful for sending and receiving "structs". The data buffers passed to these functions must be word-aligned.

Other UDN Capabilities

The UDN hardware provides a variety of capabilities beyond sending and receiving words. For more information about the UDN hardware, see arch/udn.h.

Defines

#define TMC_UDN0_DEMUX_TAG   UDN0_DEMUX_TAG
 The default tag for UDN demux queue 0.
#define TMC_UDN1_DEMUX_TAG   UDN1_DEMUX_TAG
 The default tag for UDN demux queue 1.
#define TMC_UDN2_DEMUX_TAG   UDN2_DEMUX_TAG
 The default tag for UDN demux queue 2.
#define TMC_UDN3_DEMUX_TAG   UDN3_DEMUX_TAG
 The default tag for UDN demux queue 3.

Functions

static __inline void tmc_udn_send_buffer (DynamicHeader dest, unsigned long tag, const void *buf, unsigned long words)
 Send a packet of words on the UDN.
static __inline void tmc_udn0_receive_buffer (void *buf, unsigned long words)
 Receive a packet of words from UDN demux 0.
static __inline void tmc_udn1_receive_buffer (void *buf, unsigned long words)
 Receive a packet of words from UDN demux 1.
static __inline void tmc_udn2_receive_buffer (void *buf, unsigned long words)
 Receive a packet of words from UDN demux 2.
static __inline void tmc_udn3_receive_buffer (void *buf, unsigned long words)
 Receive a packet of words from UDN demux 3.
static __USUALLY_INLINE void tmc_udn_send (uint_reg_t word)
 Inject a word into the UDN.
static __USUALLY_INLINE void tmc_udn_send_1 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0)
 Send a packet of one word.
static __USUALLY_INLINE void tmc_udn_send_2 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0, uint_reg_t n1)
 Send a packet of two words.
static __USUALLY_INLINE void tmc_udn_send_3 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0, uint_reg_t n1, uint_reg_t n2)
 Send a packet of three words.

 Send a packet of four words.

static __USUALLY_INLINE uint_reg_t tmc_udn0_receive (void)
 Receive a word from UDN demux queue 0.
static __USUALLY_INLINE uint_reg_t tmc_udn1_receive (void)
 Receive a word from UDN demux queue 1.
static __USUALLY_INLINE uint_reg_t tmc_udn2_receive (void)
 Receive a word from UDN demux queue 2.
static __USUALLY_INLINE uint_reg_t tmc_udn3_receive (void)
 Receive a word from UDN demux queue 3.
static __USUALLY_INLINE
unsigned int 		tmc_udn0_available_count (void)
 Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 0.
static __USUALLY_INLINE
unsigned int 		tmc_udn1_available_count (void)
 Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 1.
static __USUALLY_INLINE
unsigned int 		tmc_udn2_available_count (void)
 Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 2.
static __USUALLY_INLINE
unsigned int 		tmc_udn3_available_count (void)
 Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 3.
static __USUALLY_INLINE
unsigned int 		tmc_udn_available_mask (void)
 Determine which UDN demuxes have data available.

Define Documentation

#define TMC_UDN0_DEMUX_TAG   UDN0_DEMUX_TAG

The default tag for UDN demux queue 0.

#define TMC_UDN1_DEMUX_TAG   UDN1_DEMUX_TAG

The default tag for UDN demux queue 1.

#define TMC_UDN2_DEMUX_TAG   UDN2_DEMUX_TAG

The default tag for UDN demux queue 2.

#define TMC_UDN3_DEMUX_TAG   UDN3_DEMUX_TAG

The default tag for UDN demux queue 3.

Function Documentation

static __inline void tmc_udn_send_buffer ( DynamicHeader  dest,
unsigned long  tag,
const void *  buf,
unsigned long  words 
) [static]

Send a packet of words on the UDN.

Parameters:
dest The destination (with no length).
tag The tag (e.g. UDN0_DEMUX_TAG).
buf A buffer aligned to int_reg_t.
words The size of the buffer (in int_reg_t units). This function can send a maximum of 126 int_reg_t words.

static __inline void tmc_udn0_receive_buffer ( void *  buf,
unsigned long  words 
) [static]

Receive a packet of words from UDN demux 0.

Parameters:
buf A buffer aligned to int_reg_t.
words The size of the buffer (in int_reg_t units).

static __inline void tmc_udn1_receive_buffer ( void *  buf,
unsigned long  words 
) [static]

Receive a packet of words from UDN demux 1.

Parameters:
buf A buffer aligned to int_reg_t.
words The size of the buffer (in int_reg_t units).

static __inline void tmc_udn2_receive_buffer ( void *  buf,
unsigned long  words 
) [static]

Receive a packet of words from UDN demux 2.

Parameters:
buf A buffer aligned to int_reg_t.
words The size of the buffer (in int_reg_t units).

static __inline void tmc_udn3_receive_buffer ( void *  buf,
unsigned long  words 
) [static]

Receive a packet of words from UDN demux 3.

Parameters:
buf A buffer aligned to int_reg_t.
words The size of the buffer (in int_reg_t units).

static __USUALLY_INLINE void tmc_udn_send ( uint_reg_t  word  )  [static]

Inject a word into the UDN.

All packets must consist of a DynamicHeader word, followed by a tag word, followed by data words. This function should be avoided in favor of tmc_udn_send_1() and the like, which provide static compilation errors if the header length does not match the number of data words injected.

Parameters:
word The word to be sent.

static __USUALLY_INLINE void tmc_udn_send_1 ( DynamicHeader  dest,
uint32_t  tag,
uint_reg_t  n0 
) [static]

Send a packet of one word.

The parameters are a destination (with no length), a tag, and then the appropriate number of data words.

Examples:
bme/client_server/bme_server.c.

static __USUALLY_INLINE void tmc_udn_send_2 ( DynamicHeader  dest,
uint32_t  tag,
uint_reg_t  n0,
uint_reg_t  n1 
) [static]

Send a packet of two words.

The parameters are a destination (with no length), a tag, and then the appropriate number of data words.

uint32_t  tag,
uint_reg_t  n0,
uint_reg_t  n1,
uint_reg_t  n2 
) [static]

Send a packet of three words.

The parameters are a destination (with no length), a tag, and then the appropriate number of data words.

uint_reg_t  n2,
uint_reg_t  n3 
) [static]

static __USUALLY_INLINE void tmc_udn_send_20 ( DynamicHeader  dest,
uint32_t  tag,
uint_reg_t  n0,
uint_reg_t  n1,
uint_reg_t  n2,
uint_reg_t  n3,
uint_reg_t  n4,
uint_reg_t  n5,
uint_reg_t  n6,
uint_reg_t  n7,
uint_reg_t  n8,
uint_reg_t  n9,
uint_reg_t  n10,
uint_reg_t  n11,
uint_reg_t  n12,
uint_reg_t  n13,
uint_reg_t  n14,
uint_reg_t  n15,
uint_reg_t  n16,
uint_reg_t  n17,
uint_reg_t  n18,
uint_reg_t  n19 
) [static]

Send a packet of 20 words.

The parameters are a destination (with no length), a tag, and then the appropriate number of data words.

static __USUALLY_INLINE uint_reg_t tmc_udn0_receive ( void   )  [static]

Receive a word from UDN demux queue 0.

Returns:
A word from the UDN.

static __USUALLY_INLINE uint_reg_t tmc_udn1_receive ( void   )  [static]

Receive a word from UDN demux queue 1.

Returns:
A word from the UDN.

static __USUALLY_INLINE uint_reg_t tmc_udn2_receive ( void   )  [static]

Receive a word from UDN demux queue 2.

Returns:
A word from the UDN.

static __USUALLY_INLINE uint_reg_t tmc_udn3_receive ( void   )  [static]

Receive a word from UDN demux queue 3.

Returns:
A word from the UDN.

static __USUALLY_INLINE unsigned int tmc_udn0_available_count ( void   )  [static]

Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 0.

Returns:
The number of words in the demux queue.

static __USUALLY_INLINE unsigned int tmc_udn1_available_count ( void   )  [static]

Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 1.

Returns:
The number of words in the demux queue.

static __USUALLY_INLINE unsigned int tmc_udn2_available_count ( void   )  [static]

Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 2.

Returns:
The number of words in the demux queue.

static __USUALLY_INLINE unsigned int tmc_udn3_available_count ( void   )  [static]

Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 3.

Returns:
The number of words in the demux queue.

static __USUALLY_INLINE unsigned int tmc_udn_available_mask ( void   )  [static]

Determine which UDN demuxes have data available.

Returns:
A mask of "SPR_UDN_DATA_AVAIL__AVAIL_*_MASK" values, based on which UDN demuxes currently have data available.
